name: Belgian Endive and Ham Au Gratin
recipeIngredient: 1/2 to 3/4 cup grated cheddar or Gruyère cheese
1/2 to 3/4 cup fresh soft bread crumbs
1/4 cup grated cheddar or Gruyère cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the bottom of the endive heads and remove any outer leaves if necessary.
2. Cook in boiling salted water with a squeeze of lemon juice for about 15 minutes until just tender. Drain well and wrap each head of endive in a slice of ham, then place into the baking dish.
3. Prepare the sauce while the endives are cooking: Heat the butter in a saucepan. Remove from heat, stir in the flour, return to the heat and cook for several minutes. Remove from heat and blend in the milk. Stir over low heat until thickened, add seasoning and 2 tablespoons of the endive stock.
4. Add the cheese to the sauce, pour over the ham and endives.
5. Top with bread crumbs and additional cheese, then brown under the broiler.
6. Top with sliced tomatoes.
7. Serve immediately. This also makes a good hors d'oeuvre for 8. Serves 4.
